nodes.label: ∅ | live | forest
nodes.sentence: Near a vast forest lived a poor woodcutter with his wife and their two children, a boy named Hansel and a girl named Gretel.

nodes.label: they | have | enough
nodes.sentence: They had barely enough to eat, and when a great famine came over the land, the man could no longer find enough bread for their daily meal.

nodes.label: he | sigh | ∅
nodes.sentence: One night, as he lay worried in bed, he sighed and said to his wife, "What will become of us?

nodes.label: we | feed | child
nodes.sentence: How can we feed the children when there is nothing left even for ourselves?"

nodes.label: woman | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: The woman replied, "Here’s what we’ll do: early tomorrow morning, we’ll take the children deep into the forest.

nodes.label: we | light | fire
nodes.sentence: We’ll light a fire, give each a last piece of bread, then leave and go back to work.

nodes.label: they | find | way
nodes.sentence: They won’t find their way home, and we’ll be rid of them."

nodes.label: they | tear | piece
nodes.sentence: "No," the man protested, "I cannot abandon my children to the forest—they will be torn to pieces by wild animals."

nodes.label: you | fool | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Oh, you fool!"

nodes.label: woman | snap | ∅
nodes.sentence: the woman snapped.

nodes.label: difference | ’s | what
nodes.sentence: "If we starve together, what’s the difference?

nodes.label: we | build | coffin
nodes.sentence: We might as well build our own coffins."

nodes.label: she | nag | he
nodes.sentence: She nagged him until he agreed, though he felt sorry for the children.

nodes.label: hansel | overheard | plan
nodes.sentence: Hansel and Gretel, too hungry to sleep, overheard their stepmother’s plan.

nodes.label: gretel | cry | ∅
nodes.sentence: Gretel cried, "It’s all over for us."

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Don't worry," Hansel said.

nodes.label: i | find | way
nodes.sentence: "I’ll find a way to help us."

nodes.label: he | get | ∅
nodes.sentence: When their parents were asleep, he quietly got out of bed, put on his little coat, and slipped outside.

nodes.label: moonlight | make | pebble
nodes.sentence: The bright moonlight made the white pebbles in front of the house shine like silver coins.

nodes.label: hansel | gather | many
nodes.sentence: Hansel gathered as many as he could into his pocket and went back inside.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Sleep well, Gretel," he said.

nodes.label: god | abandon | we
nodes.sentence: "God won’t abandon us.

nodes.label: woman | call | child
nodes.sentence:
"

At dawn, before the sun rose, the woman called the children from bed.

nodes.label: ∅ | get | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Get up, lazybones!

nodes.label: we | go | forest
nodes.sentence: We’re going to the forest to gather wood."

nodes.label: she | hand | piece
nodes.sentence: She handed each a small piece of bread and warned them not to eat it all before the midday meal.

nodes.label: gretel | hide | bread
nodes.sentence: Gretel hid her bread under her apron, and Hansel kept his pebbles safe in his pocket.

nodes.label: four | set | ∅
nodes.sentence: The four set off together.

nodes.label: hansel | keep | ∅
nodes.sentence: After walking a short distance, Hansel kept stopping and looking back at the house.

nodes.label: father | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: His father asked, "Why do you lag behind?"

nodes.label: hansel | lie | ∅
nodes.sentence: "I’m watching my little white cat sitting on the roof, saying goodbye," Hansel lied.

nodes.label: stepmother | say | fool
nodes.sentence: "Fool," his stepmother said.

nodes.label: it | ’ | sun
nodes.sentence: "That’s not a cat; it’s the morning sun shining on the chimney."

nodes.label: hansel | drop | pebble
nodes.sentence: But Hansel was secretly dropping pebbles along the path.

nodes.label: father | tell | child
nodes.sentence: When they reached the heart of the forest, the father told the children to gather wood while he lit a fire.

nodes.label: hansel | pile | brush
nodes.sentence: Hansel and Gretel piled brush high like a small hill.

nodes.label: we | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the fire blazed, the woman told them, "Rest by the fire, we’ll go fetch more wood and come back for you.

nodes.label: child | sit | flame
nodes.sentence:
"

The children sat by the flames and at noon ate a small piece of bread.

nodes.label: they | hear | ∅
nodes.sentence: They heard what sounded like the ax chopping wood and thought their father was close.

nodes.label: it | be | branch
nodes.sentence: But it was only a branch tied to a dead tree swaying in the wind.

nodes.label: child | fall | set
nodes.sentence: The children, tired, fell asleep as the sun set.

nodes.label: gretel | wake | cry
nodes.sentence: At night, Gretel woke crying, "How will we get out now?"

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Wait for the moon," Hansel said.

nodes.label: he | take | hand
nodes.sentence: When it rose full and bright, he took Gretel’s hand and followed the shining pebbles to find their way home.

nodes.label: they | reach | house
nodes.sentence: After walking all night, they reached their house at dawn.

nodes.label: woman | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The woman was angry, scolding them for sleeping so long in the forest.

nodes.label: father | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Their father was happy, for leaving them had broken his heart.

nodes.label: famine | strike | ∅
nodes.sentence: Soon another famine struck, and again the children heard their stepmother saying, "We have but half a loaf left; the children must go deeper into the forest where they can’t return."

nodes.label: heart | ache | ∅
nodes.sentence: The man’s heart ached, but the woman was relentless, and he gave in once more.

nodes.label: hansel | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: This time, Hansel tried to gather pebbles again, but the door was locked.

nodes.label: god | help | we
nodes.sentence: He comforted Gretel, "Don’t cry, God will help us.

nodes.label: woman | give | piece
nodes.sentence:
"

The next morning, the woman gave them even smaller pieces of bread.

nodes.label: hansel | crumble | bread
nodes.sentence: Hansel, unable to collect pebbles, crumbled his bread and dropped crumbs along the way.

nodes.label: he | pause | ∅
nodes.sentence: Again, he paused often to look back.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "I’m watching my little pigeon goodbye on the roof," he said.

nodes.label: fool | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Fool!" said his stepmother.

nodes.label: that | ’ | sun
nodes.sentence: "That’s the morning sun, not a pigeon.

nodes.label: woman | lead | they
nodes.sentence:
"

The woman led them deeper into the forest, to a place they had never been.

nodes.label: she | light | fire
nodes.sentence: She lit a fire and told them to sit and rest while she went for wood.

nodes.label: gretel | share | bread
nodes.sentence: At noon, Gretel shared her tiny bread with Hansel, who had nothing left.

nodes.label: they | fall | asleep
nodes.sentence: They fell asleep, but no one returned.

nodes.label: hansel | tell | gretel
nodes.sentence: After dark, Hansel told Gretel to wait for the moon so they could follow the crumbs.

nodes.label: bird | eat | they
nodes.sentence: But when they tried, the crumbs were gone—birds had eaten them all.

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "We’ll find our way," Hansel said bravely, but they wandered all night and all the next day, growing weak from hunger.

nodes.label: they | find | berry
nodes.sentence: They found only a few berries, lay down under a tree, and fell asleep.

nodes.label: they | set | ∅
nodes.sentence: Three days after leaving home, they set off again, but only went deeper into the forest.

nodes.label: they | see | sing
nodes.sentence: Near midday, they saw a beautiful white bird singing on a bough.

nodes.label: it | fly | ∅
nodes.sentence: After it finished, it flew ahead, leading them to a small house with a roof of bread, walls of cake, and windows of clear sugar.

nodes.label: we | eat | this
nodes.sentence: "We’ll eat this!"

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Hansel said eagerly, breaking off a piece of roof.

nodes.label: gretel | nibble | pane
nodes.sentence: Gretel nibbled a sugar window pane.

nodes.label: child | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Suddenly, a voice called from inside:

"Nibble, nibble, gnaw, who’s nibbling at my little house?"

"The wind, the wind, the heaven-born wind," the children replied, ignoring the warning and eating more.

nodes.label: door | swing | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then the door swung open, and an old woman with crutches came out.

nodes.label: hansel | drop | treat
nodes.sentence: Hansel and Gretel dropped their treats, frightened.

nodes.label: she | smile | ∅
nodes.sentence: She smiled kindly and said, "Dear children, who brought you here?

nodes.label: ∅ | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Come in and stay with me.

nodes.label: harm | come | you
nodes.sentence: No harm will come to you."

nodes.label: she | hold | hand
nodes.sentence: She held their hands and led them inside, where she served them milk, pancakes, sugar, apples, and nuts.

nodes.label: she | make | bed
nodes.sentence: She made two cozy beds for them, and the children felt they were in heaven.

nodes.label: woman | be | witch
nodes.sentence: But the woman was actually a wicked witch who trapped children to eat them.

nodes.label: she | build | house
nodes.sentence: She had built the house to lure them in.

nodes.label: witch | have | eye
nodes.sentence: Witches have red eyes and sharp noses, sensing when humans are near.

nodes.label: she | laugh | ∅
nodes.sentence: When Hansel and Gretel came close, she laughed wickedly, "I have them now; they shall not escape!"

nodes.label: she | lock | hansel
nodes.sentence: Early the next morning, before the children woke, she locked Hansel in a tiny stable with a grated door.

nodes.label: he | scream | ∅
nodes.sentence: He screamed and struggled, but it was no use.

nodes.label: she | wake | gretel
nodes.sentence: She woke Gretel, shaking her, and ordered, "Get up, lazy girl!

nodes.label: he | need | ∅
nodes.sentence: He needs to be fattened up for my cooking."

nodes.label: gretel | sob | ∅
nodes.sentence: Gretel sobbed but had to obey.

nodes.label: hansel | feed | food
nodes.sentence: Hansel was fed the best food, while Gretel got only crab shells.

nodes.label: witch | stick | finger
nodes.sentence: Every morning the witch stuck her finger through the bars to feel if Hansel was fat yet.

nodes.label: hansel | hold | bone
nodes.sentence: Hansel cleverly held out a thin bone instead, fooling her poor eyesight.

nodes.label: week | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence: Weeks passed, and Hansel remained thin.

nodes.label: witch | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: The witch grew impatient and threatened, "Fat or thin, tomorrow I’ll kill and cook him."

nodes.label: gretel | cry | ∅
nodes.sentence: Gretel cried, begging God for help, wishing they’d been eaten by forest beasts so they could have died together.

nodes.label: witch | make | ∅
nodes.sentence: One morning, the witch made Gretel stoke the oven’s fire and ordered her to check if it was hot enough to bake bread.

nodes.label: she | push | gretel
nodes.sentence: She pushed Gretel toward the blazing oven, planning to shut her inside and cook her too.

nodes.label: gretel | pretend | ∅
nodes.sentence: But Gretel pretended not to understand.

nodes.label: i | get | ∅
nodes.sentence: "How do I get in?"

nodes.label: she | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: she asked.

nodes.label: witch | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "The door is big enough," the witch said, crawling in herself to show her.

nodes.label: gretel | shove | witch
nodes.sentence: Quick as a flash, Gretel shoved the witch deep into the oven, closed the iron door, and locked it tight.

nodes.label: witch | scream | ∅
nodes.sentence: The witch screamed horribly as she burned.

nodes.label: gretel | run | stable
nodes.sentence: Gretel ran to the stable and freed Hansel.

nodes.label: we | save | ∅
nodes.sentence: "We’re saved!

nodes.label: witch | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The witch is dead!"

nodes.label: she | cry | ∅
nodes.sentence: she cried.

nodes.sentence: Hansel flew out like a bird from a cage.

nodes.label: they | hug | ∅
nodes.sentence: They hugged, danced, and kissed with joy.

nodes.sentence: Freed, they explored the witch’s house and found chests filled with pearls and jewels.

nodes.sentence: Hansel stuffed his pockets with treasures, and Gretel filled her apron.

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "Now we must leave," Hansel said, "and get out of the witch’s forest.

After two hours, they came to a wide river.

nodes.label: hansel | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: "We can’t cross—no plank or bridge," Hansel said.

nodes.label: ∅ | look | ∅

She called:

"Little duck, little duck, can you see
Hansel and Gretel waiting for thee?

nodes.label: ∅ | take | we
nodes.sentence:
No plank or bridge is anywhere near,
Take us across on your back so dear.

nodes.label: duck | come | ∅

The duck came over.

nodes.sentence: Hansel sat on her back, then Gretel, but she was too heavy for both at once.

nodes.sentence: So the duck carried them one at a time safely across.

nodes.label: forest | grow | side
nodes.sentence: Once on the other side, the forest grew familiar, and soon they saw their father’s house.

nodes.label: they | run | ∅
nodes.sentence: They ran inside and threw themselves into his arms.

nodes.label: stepmother | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Their father had never known a happy moment since abandoning them; their stepmother, however, was dead.

nodes.label: trouble | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Their troubles were over, and they lived happily together forever.

nodes.sentence: The story ends here—there runs a mouse; whoever catches it, may make a big fur cap from it.
